questions.prompt: When stacking operators on a 500-result query, how do you know which constraint did the real work?
options: Whichever operator you typed first always matters most
Read the drop in result count after each operator you add
The longest operator string is always the most precise
You can't tell without contacting an administrator
questions.explanation: Add one operator at a time and watch the count fall; the biggest delta shows which constraint removed the most noise.

questions.prompt: You're exploring an unfamiliar result set and don't yet know which apps have hits. What's the better tool?
options: Type the app: operator from memory
Click datasource facets to see what's actually there
Add more keywords until it narrows
Save the query before running it
questions.explanation: Facets are for discovery — they show you what's present. Typed operators win when you already know and want a repeatable string.

questions.prompt: A trusted document ranks at #8. What's the honest way to investigate the ranking?
options: Look up the exact published signal weights and recompute the score
Change one signal at a time and observe whether the rank moves
Assume recency is always weighted highest
Conclude the graph is broken
questions.explanation: Exact ranking weights aren't published. Test one signal per change and read the observed rank movement rather than guessing weights.

questions.prompt: What three things must a written search recipe include so a teammate reproduces your exact result set?
options: The screenshot, the date, and your username
The exact operator string, its purpose, and when to re-run it
A description of the UI buttons to click
The number of results and the top hit's title
questions.explanation: A recipe is the exact operator string plus the context — its purpose and re-run cadence — so anyone can run it and get the same set.

questions.prompt: Which question best justifies Glean's multi-step thinking mode over a quick answer?
options: What is our PTO accrual rate?
Summarize this one document
Compare our three vendor SLAs and flag the weakest term
Who owns the launch deck?
questions.explanation: Comparing several sources and then making a judgment is synthesis — the case where a deeper multi-step pass earns its added latency.
